_description: ai-pilot - APEX context injection + caching, delegated to @fusengine/harness (scope 'aipilot'). Routing is by hook event + agent matcher; the harness dispatches internally. The UserPromptSubmit APEX injection is NOT duplicated here — core-guards already injects the APEX preamble + CLAUDE.md on UserPromptSubmit (the richer ai-pilot variant was merged into the harness's buildApexInstruction). Enforcement (bash-write, APEX gates, SOLID file-size, doc-consulted) is delegated to the harness via core-guards.

SubagentStart._description: Single matcher-'' entry: the harness dispatcher (dispatch-aipilot.ts onSubagentStart) already combines apex + lessons + the agent-type-specific cache (explore/doc/test) in ONE call, routing by agent_type internally. Extra per-agent entries here would match ALONGSIDE this one and inject the same context twice.

PreToolUse._description: Harness: inject APEX context into Task sub-agent prompts when .claude/apex/ exists. Resolves the harness binary from the root plugins/node_modules (single install).

SubagentStop._description: Single matcher-'' entry (Python parity: check-solid-from-transcript ran with matcher '' for EVERY agent). The dispatcher (onSubagentStop) routes internally: research-expert -> doc cache, sniper -> lessons + test caches, then the universal SOLID transcript check for all agents. Per-agent entries here would both narrow the SOLID check AND double-fire the cache writers.

PostToolUse._description: Write|Edit reaches checkSolidCompliance (Python parity: check-solid-compliance.py matcher 'Write|Edit'); TaskCreate|TaskUpdate reaches syncTaskTracking. Same dispatcher (aipilotPostToolUse) routes by tool_name.
